rationale: The page contains statistical data on Indian school education infrastructure and gender representation, followed by detailed information on three major initiatives: the MERITE scheme for technical education, the WHO's Global Network of Age-Friendly Cities and Communities (GNAFCC) with a focus on Kozhikode, and the Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) including recent global and national findings.
cleanedText: Data and Trends in Indian School Education in percentage terms (2022-23 to 2024-25). Infrastructure Facilities: Computer access (2023-24: 57.2, 2024-25: 64.7); Internet (2023-24: 53.9, 2024-25: 63.5); Girls Toilet (2023-24: 97.2, 2024-25: 97.3). Female Representation: Girls Enrolment (2023-24: 48.1, 2024-25: 48.3); Female Teachers (2023-24: 53.3, 2024-25: 54.2). 8.18.6. MULTIDISCIPLINARY EDUCATION AND RESEARCH IMPROVEMENT IN TECHNICAL EDUCATION (MERITE) SCHEME. Union Cabinet approved Budgetary Support for MERITE Scheme. About MERITE Scheme: Type: Central Sector Scheme; Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Education; Objective: To improve the quality, equity and governance in technical education covering all States/UTs. Aligned with National Education Policy 2020; Budget: Rs. 4200 crore for a period from 2025-26 to 2029-30. Includes external assistance of Rs. 2100 crore from World Bank as loan; Beneficiaries: 275 technical institutions comprising 175 engineering institutions and 100 polytechnics. 8.18.7. GLOBAL NETWORK OF AGE-FRIENDLY CITIES AND COMMUNITIES (GNAFCC). Kozhikode city has been honoured with a membership in the WHO’s GNAFCC. About GNAFCC: Established: 2010; Members: 1300 members from 51 countries; Aim: To connect cities, communities and organizations worldwide with the common vision of making their communities great places to grow older in; Objectives: Inspire change by showing what can be done and how; Connect cities and communities worldwide to facilitate the exchange of information, knowledge and experience; Support cities and communities to find appropriate innovative and evidence-based solutions. 8.18.8. UNIVERSAL IMMUNIZATION PROGRAMME (UIP). Recently, WHO/UNICEF 2024 provides the world’s largest and most comprehensive dataset on immunization trends for vaccinations against 14 diseases. Key Findings: Global: In 2024, 89% of infants globally received at least one dose of the diphtheria, tetanus and pertussis (DTP) containing vaccine; India: Reduced its number of zero-dose children by 43% in 2024 (from 1.6 million in 2023 to 0.9 million in 2024). Zero-dose children are those who have not received a single vaccine. Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) of India: Genesis: Initially launched in 1978 as Expanded Programme on Immunization, it was rebranded as UIP in 1985; Coverage: Provides free immunization against 12 diseases.
